title: Outbound address is broken under rootless containers
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

When creating a rootless container, if I set outbound_addr as described in the podman run docs, doesn't matter if I set an IP or an interface it doesn't seem to work and instead every direct IP curl request hangs forever within the container.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a network interface with internet connectivity and an outbound IP. I have created a wireguard interface called `wg0`.

2. Start up a test container with the following flags: `--dns 1.1.1.1 --network slirp4netns:port_handler=slirp4netns,outbound_addr=wg0,enable_ipv6=false`. Such as `podman run -it --name test_container --dns 1.1.1.1 --network slirp4netns:port_handler=slirp4netns,outbound_addr=wg0,enable_ipv6=false alpine /bin/ash`

3. Try to install curl and send a request to a direct IP address, such as: `curl http://1.1.1.1`

**Describe the results you received:**

The terminal hangs until I manually interrupt. There doesn't seem to be networking within the container at all.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The internet works within the container and the above mentioned curl command doesn't hang.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Before submitting this issue I have confirmed that the interface works and outside of the container I can use it just fine:
```
[test@machine ~]$ curl --silent -q --interface wg0 http://1.1.1.1 -I | head -n1
HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Built:        Wed May 12 22:27:59 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
>     1. Create a network interface with internet connectivity and an outbound IP. I have created a wireguard interface called `wg0`.

is the wg0 created on the host?  The rootless container  cannot access devices from the host network namespace

It is, indeed. Why can it not access that? If I run curl for example from the same user on the host, it can just use the specific outbound IP/inteface.

But if that would be the case a warning would be helpful instead of silently failing.
sorry my last comment, I misunderstood the original post.
can you check what connection is performed?  What does `netstat -aenp | grep slirp4netns` show?

This is all I am seeing: 
```
[test@machine ~]$ netstat -aenp | grep slirp4netns
(Not all processes could be identified, non-owned process info
 will not be shown, you would have to be root to see it all.)
udp        0      0 10.65.203.10:39189      0.0.0.0:*                           1000       82515613   711710/slirp4netns
udp        0      0 10.65.203.10:53886      0.0.0.0:*                           1000       82515603   711710/slirp4netns
unix  2      [ ACC ]     STREAM     LISTENING     82511542 711710/slirp4netns   /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/199b4a78e6ec49163e5f2e8c83db15f665695e770d5439748c256d9c98179450.net
unix  2      [ ]         STREAM     CONNECTED     82511541 711710/slirp4netns
```

There is only one container running at the moment under this user.

Surprisingly 10.65.203.10 is the IP address of my wg0 interface.
I did some further debugging and if I do `tcpdump -i wg0` there is absolutely nothing I can see. It looks like the packets are never even leaving the container.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I will just close this and see if slirp4netns will ever fix the issue in the other ticket opened at them.
